questions.question: What is the primary scope of an Organization Owner's authority?

options.text: To manage billing and payments for the entire organization
options.explanation: While organization owners handle billing, their unique authority goes beyond payments to include managing workspaces and members centrally.

options.text: To manage all workspaces and members in one central place and apply settings across the board
options.explanation: Organization owners can view and manage multiple workspaces as one organization and apply settings universally.

options.text: To manage the security settings of a single workspace
options.explanation: Organization owners manage the organization level, not just a single workspace's security settings.

options.text: To delete workspaces but not create new ones
options.explanation: Organization owners manage settings and views, but the specific ability to delete a workspace is attributed to Workspace Owners.

questions.question: What specific limitation applies to Membership Admins regarding workspace settings?

options.text: They cannot add new members to the workspace at all
options.explanation: Membership admins *can* add members to the workspace, but only in a 'member role'.

options.text: They can edit security settings but not member groups
options.explanation: They can manage groups, but they cannot edit other workspace settings.

options.text: They cannot edit other workspace settings
options.explanation: Membership admins have limited permissions; specifically, they cannot modify the general settings of the workspace itself.

options.text: They can edit any setting except for workspace ownership transfer
options.explanation: They lack permission to edit workspace settings entirely, not just the ownership transfer.

questions.question: Which role is capable of deleting an entire workspace?

options.text: Organization Owner
options.explanation: Organization owners manage settings across workspaces but do not have the specific authority to delete individual workspaces.

options.text: Membership Admin
options.explanation: Membership admins can only add or remove members and manage groups; they cannot delete workspaces.

options.text: Workspace Owner
options.explanation: Workspace owners can manage everything in the workspace, including the ability to delete it entirely.

options.text: No role can delete a workspace
options.explanation: The Workspace Owner role specifically has the expansive permissions required to delete a workspace.

questions.question: When a Membership Admin adds a user to the workspace, what role must the user be assigned?

options.text: Workspace Owner
options.explanation: Membership admins cannot add workspace owners to the workspace.

options.text: Membership Admin
options.explanation: Membership admins cannot add other membership admins to the workspace.

options.text: Member
options.explanation: Membership admins can only add users in a 'member role only'.

options.text: Guest
options.explanation: The article specifies the 'member role' specifically, not guests.

questions.question: Which of the following actions is a Membership Admin permitted to perform regarding groups?

options.text: Delete the entire group
options.explanation: While they can add and remove members from groups, deleting the group itself is not explicitly mentioned as a specific permission.

options.text: Add or remove members, other membership admins, and workspace owners from existing groups
options.explanation: Membership admins have permission to manage the composition of groups by adding or removing members and other admins.

options.text: Create new groups without permission
options.explanation: They can only add or remove from *existing* groups, not create new ones.

options.text: Edit the group description
options.explanation: Since they cannot edit other workspace settings, editing group descriptions (which are settings) would fall under this restriction.

questions.question: How does a Workspace Owner differ from a Membership Admin in terms of permissions?

options.text: Workspace Owners can only manage security settings
options.explanation: Workspace owners manage everything in the workspace (settings, security, members, groups), whereas Membership admins have very limited scope.

options.text: Membership Admins can delete workspaces
options.explanation: Membership Admins cannot delete workspaces; only Workspace Owners have that level of authority.

options.text: Workspace Owners can manage everything in the workspace from settings and security to members and groups
options.explanation: Workspace owners are described as having the most expansive permissions, covering the entire workspace's lifecycle.

options.text: Membership Admins can add users to the workspace in any role
options.explanation: Membership Admins are restricted to adding users only in the 'member role'.

questions.question: Which of the following is explicitly forbidden for a Membership Admin to do within the workspace?

options.text: Add users to groups
options.explanation: They are allowed to add users to groups.

options.text: Remove users from groups
options.explanation: They are allowed to remove users from groups.

options.text: Add a workspace owner to the workspace
options.explanation: The article explicitly states: 'Membership admins cannot add workspace owners or membership admins to the workspace.'

options.text: Add users to the workspace
options.explanation: They are allowed to add users to the workspace, provided they are assigned the 'member' role.

questions.question: What is the definition of a Workspace Owner's role?

options.text: A user who manages billing and subscriptions
options.explanation: Billing management is typically separate or handled by Org Owners, whereas Workspace Owners manage the operational aspects of the workspace.

options.text: The primary administrator of a workspace with the most expansive permissions
options.explanation: The article describes them as the primary administrator with expansive permissions covering settings, security, members, and groups.

options.text: A user who can view all workspaces in the organization
options.explanation: Viewing all workspaces is the specific duty of an Organization Owner.

options.text: A user who can only manage member groups
options.explanation: They manage everything, not just groups.

questions.question: Can a Membership Admin add another Membership Admin to the workspace?

options.text: Yes, they can add any user role they want
options.explanation: Membership admins cannot add other membership admins to the workspace.

options.text: No, they can only add regular members
options.explanation: The article explicitly states that Membership admins cannot add workspace owners or other membership admins to the workspace.

options.text: Yes, provided they have explicit permission from an Organization Owner
options.explanation: The article does not mention permission overrides; the restriction is inherent to the role.

options.text: Only if the other admin is also a Workspace Owner
options.explanation: The restriction applies to other membership admins regardless of their status.

questions.question: Organization owners are able to apply settings where?

options.text: Only to the billing settings of the organization
options.explanation: Organization owners have wide-ranging control, not limited to billing.

options.text: Only to the security settings of a single workspace
options.explanation: They have organization-wide control.

options.text: Across the board (organization-wide)
options.explanation: Organization owners can apply settings across all workspaces and members in the organization.

options.text: Nowhere, settings must be applied by individual Workspace Owners
options.explanation: Organization owners are specifically granted the ability to apply settings across the board.
